data.title: Nexans reported strong 2025 results: €6.1B sales, 8.3% organic growth, €728M EBITDA (+27.3%), €358M net income, €344M FCF, €2.90 per share dividend. M&A activity to increase.
data.description: In 2025, Nexans reported strong financial performance with a remarkable +8.3% organic growth and an Adjusted EBITDA margin increase to 11.9% of standard sales. The company entered into exclusive negotiations for the sale of Autoelectric and had a solid balance sheet, enabling enhanced M&A activity. The 2025 guidance was adjusted to align with the new scope of operations, excluding discontinued operations, with Adjusted EBITDA between €710 million and €760 million and Free Cash Flow between €275 million and €375 million. The company saw strong results, including a net income from continuing operations at €219 million (up +31.1% from 2024) and a dividend increase of +11.5%. Nexans highlighted its M&A strategy for growth, sustainability efforts in line with the E3 performance model, and provided guidance for 2026, expecting Adjusted EBITDA between €730 million and €810 million and Free Cash Flow between €210 million and €310 million, with H1 2026 expected to be softer compared to H2 2026.
Nestlé to merge its nutrition segment into the Nestlé Health Science business.
Nestlé outlines strategy to concentrate on coffee, pet care, nutrition, and food & snacks.
Nestlé begins formal engagement with potential partners for its Waters business, targeting consolidation by 2027.

data.title: Teck Resources Limited reports unaudited Q4 2025 results with $1.5 billion Adjusted EBITDA and $671 million adjusted profit. Proposed merger with Anglo American advanced, aiming to create a global top-five copper company. QB Action Plan saw improvement, including $792 million profit from continuing ops. Copper segment gross profit hit $1.1 billion, zinc at $305 million. Annual High-Potential Incident rate improved to 0.06. Total cash unit costs for copper and zinc within ranges, net cash unit costs at $2.67 per pound. Annual production guidance remained the same for 2026. Teck named one of Canada's Top 100 Employers for the ninth year. Use of non-GAAP financial measures disclosed for understanding performance.
data.description: Teck Resources Limited announced strong unaudited fourth quarter results for 2025, citing a robust financial performance mainly due to higher copper prices. The proposed merger with Anglo American plc progressed well with shareholder approval and government clearance. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $1.5 billion, a $678 million increase from last year, and profit from continuing operations before taxes was $792 million. Teck ended the year in a net cash position with $1.3 billion cash flow from operations in Q4 2025. Copper prices were at US$5.67 per pound at year end, driving gross profit from the copper segment to $747 million. The QB Action Plan aimed at ramping up production with improved sand drainage rates and dam development, achieving a net cash unit cost for 2025 of US$2.67 per pound. Teck's safety record improved with an annual High-Potential Incident frequency rate of 0.06. The Merger with Anglo American is expected to yield approximately US$800 million in synergies, 80% realized by the end of the second year post-completion. Teck reaffirmed its 2026 annual guidance with copper net cash unit costs of US$1.85-$2.20 per pound and zinc net cash unit costs of US$0.65-$0.75 per pound. Teck was named one of Canada's Top 100 Employers for the ninth consecutive year.
Nestlé announces Anna Mohl to leave Executive Board on 28 February 2026; she is CEO of Nestlé Health Science.

data.title: Valneva reports preliminary unaudited 2025 revenue and cash results, provides 2026 outlook. Stock symbols: Nasdaq: VALN, Euronext Paris: VLA.
data.description: In Lyon, France on February 19, 2026, Valneva SE, a specialty vaccine company listed on Nasdaq: VALN and Euronext Paris: VLA, disclosed its initial unaudited revenue and cash figures for the fiscal year 2025. It also offered an early projection for the year 2026.

data.title: Aegon reveals 2025 second half year financial results.
data.description: In the latest report, it was stated that the company's revenue had increased by 15% compared to last year, reaching a total of $1.5 billion. Additionally, the company's net profit saw a significant boost of 25%, amounting to $200 million. The company attributed this growth to a rise in sales of their latest products and successful marketing strategies. This positive financial performance has led to an increase in the company's stock price by 10% in the last quarter. Furthermore, the company announced plans to invest $100 million in expanding their production facilities in order to meet the growing demand for their products.

data.title: Remitly Global (NASDAQ: RELY) stock surged 20.87% in after-hours trading to $16.45 post announcing Q4 2025 results. Q4 revenue rose 26% to $442.2 million, net income was $41.2 million vs. loss of $5.7 million a year prior. Active customers increased by 19% to 9.3 million, and send volume rose by 35% to $20.8 billion. For the full-year 2025, revenue was up 29% to $1.6 billion, adjusted EBITDA rose 93% to $272.2 million, and net income was $67.9 million vs. a loss of $37 million in 2024.
data.description: Remitly Global Inc. (NASDAQ: RELY) saw a significant surge of 20.87% in after-hours trading to $16.45 following the release of their fourth-quarter and annual results for the period ending December 31, 2025, and the announcement of a CEO transition. In Q4 2025, the company achieved its strongest quarter on record, with a 26% year-over-year increase in revenue to $442.2 million. Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization also saw a substantial growth, rising by 98% to $88.6 million. Net income stood at $41.2 million, representing a turnaround from the $5.7 million net loss reported in the previous year. Remitly Global highlighted a 19% increase in active customers, totaling 9.3 million, and a significant 35% surge in send volume to $20.8 billion. For the full-year 2025, the company reported a 29% increase in revenue to $1.6 billion, while adjusted EBITDA nearly doubled, reaching $272.2 million, a 93% increase. Net income for the year amounted to $67.9 million, in contrast to a net loss of $37 million in 2024. These figures showcased a profitable year for Remitly Global, further emphasizing its growth and financial performance.
